items.title: Skiete Willy Podcast Extra: 'PSV moet tegen Bayern soms pragmatischer zijn'
items.summary: Clubwatcher Tim Reedijk en Marco Timmer hebben het in deze Skiete Willy Podcast Extra onder meer over de cruciale Champions League-wedstrijd van PSV tegen Bayern München. Maar de mannen bespreken ook de contractsituatie van Peter Bosz, het mogelijke vertrek van Ricardo Pepi en de fitheid van de PSV-selectie.

items.title: Arteta kondigt 'ander soort werk' aan voor Timber in relaxte week voor Arsenal
items.summary: Jurriën Timber krijgt woensdag een snipperavond tijdens Arsenal – Kairat Almaty. Het laatste duel in de Champions League-hoofdfase is voor de Gunners van ondergeschikt belang, op grond van een luxepositie op de ranglijst.

items.title: Champions League news: Guardiola gives little away on Haaland’s drought

items.title: As 'managers' disappear from the Premier League, are clubs better off?
items.summary: Premier League clubs are corporations that have outgrown the "manager" role that legendary Arsenal and Man United clubs once had. Why?

items.title: Ajax ook scherp op standen andere velden: 'Daar is wel iemand mee bezig'
items.summary: Hoewel de focus vooral ligt op de eigen wedstrijd tegen Olympiakos Piraeus, zal Ajax eveneens de tussenstanden op de andere velden in de Champions League in de gaten houden. Dat vertelde trainer Fred Grim op de persconferentie.

items.title: Volgende horde genomen: Zinchenko stap dichter bij Ajax
items.summary: De komst van Oleksandr Zinchenko naar Ajax is weer een flinke stap dichterbij. Het huurcontract tussen Arsenal en Nottingham Forest is ontbonden, waardoor de Oekraïner nu vrij is om de transfer naar Amsterdam te maken.

items.title: Guardiola apologises to ref Hallam but 'defends' outburst
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has apologised to new Premier League referee Farai Hallam if he "feels offended", having taken issue with the official following Manchester City's victory over Wolves on Saturday. But the Spaniard said he has to "defend my club and my players".

items.title: Ferrari and Red Bull take on rain-hit testing in Barcelona
items.summary: Watch the morning session of day two of the shakedown as teams run their cars at the Barcelona test.

items.title: Coutinho advice & Adriano comparison - Bournemouth's new Brazilian teen
items.summary: Bournemouth have made 19-year-old Brazilian forward Rayan their second most-expensive signing. Who is he and how good can he be?

items.title: Concurrent voor Flekken, West Ham naar verluidt rond met Adama Traoré
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Spain to host 2030 World Cup final, federation says
items.summary: Spain "will be the leader of the 2030 World Cup and the final of that World Cup will be held here" says the president of the country's football federation.

items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: City's Doku still not happy about Dalot tackle
items.summary: Jérémy Doku has said "you know my feelings" when asked for his opinion of the knee-high challenge from Diogo Dalot during Manchester United's derby victory.

items.title: Fermín extends Barça deal, ending Chelsea bid
items.summary: Barcelona midfielder Fermín López has agreed a contract extension until 2031, sources have confirmed to ESPN, ending any Chelsea interest in signing the Spain international for now.

items.title: Newcastle latest: Flight to Paris delayed ahead of PSG tie
West Ham latest: Traore medical, Kinksy interest and Paqueta exit talks
Blow for Manchester United and Carrick with Dorgu facing lengthy spell out
items.summary: Danish midfielder having tests on hamstring injury 1958 fans’ group to protest before Sunday’s Fulham game Manchester United fear Patrick Dorgu could be out for a prolonged period because of the muscle injury he sustained in Sunday’s 3-2 win at Arsenal, in what would be a blow to Michael Carrick’s resurgent side. While tests are still to confirm the prognosis there is concern the Dane has a hamstring problem that will cause him to miss several weeks, with some reports stating his absence may be more than two months. Dorgu has been a key factor in United’s upturn, scoring in each of Carrick’s opening two matches. His goal against Arsenal came after an equally important one in United’s 2-0 victory over Manchester City the previous weekend. He also scored the winner in the 1-0 Boxing Day victory over Newcastle, making it three goals in his last seven appearances. Continue reading...

items.title: The first Women’s Champions Cup: what is it and should we pay attention?
items.summary: This newsletter looks at Fifa’s new competition, which takes place in London this week and has a $2.3m prize fund This week London will take centre stage as the inaugural Women’s Champions Cup, the brand-new club competition in women’s football, comes to its conclusion. Four continental champions – Arsenal, Gotham FC, Corinthians and AS Far – will meet in Brentford on Wednesday for a place in the final, which will be held at the Emirates Stadium on Sunday. With $2.3m (£1.68m) in prize money and a shiny new trophy on the line, it is far from insignificant for the teams involved. For the wider public, however, there remains a lack of understanding about what it really is and how it came to be introduced into an already crowded football space. Continue reading...

items.title: Man Utd's Dorgu set for weeks out with hamstring issue
items.summary: In-form Patrick Dorgu is still being assessed but he is likely to miss Manchester United matches with the injury he picked up against Arsenal.
